entry.title: [TECHBROS] 🧢 The Tech Bros Summer Show 🧢
entry.summary:
Bros,
We're Back.
We've been busy with our various gigs that actually pay money, but we know that you miss us... And we're coming back for a Very Special Summer Edition tomorrow.
Mary has landed the interview she's been pushing for for YEARS: a Q-and-A with Lauren herself, focusing on - what else - all the shenanigans she's been up to over on Twitter.
Your assignment: catch up on Lauren's Twitter feed with a focus on the Snowflake-related insanity (customer data hacks, Iceberg, and what exactly is going on around the broader data landscape - including the recent acquisitions of Tabular and Rockset).
But we'll start with the How and Why of Lauren's Very Important New Title: 🇺🇸 America’s Top Chief Data Analytics Engineering Field CTO 🇺🇸
As always, bring your questions, your rants, and your latest insights into what I still consider the tech story of the year (is Kate Middleton an AI 🤔).
🍺 See you Friday, Bros 🍺
entry.content:
Bros,

We're Back.

We've been busy with our various gigs that actually pay money, but we know that you miss us... And we're coming back for a Very Special Summer Edition tomorrow.

Mary has landed the interview she's been pushing for for YEARS: a Q-and-A with Lauren herself, focusing on - what else - all the shenanigans she's been up to over on Twitter.

Your assignment: catch up on Lauren's Twitter feed with a focus on the Snowflake-related insanity (customer data hacks, Iceberg, and what exactly is going on around the broader data landscape - including the recent acquisitions of Tabular and Rockset).

But we'll start with the How and Why of Lauren's Very Important New Title: 🇺🇸 America’s Top Chief Data Analytics Engineering Field CTO 🇺🇸

As always, bring your questions, your rants, and your latest insights into what I still consider the tech story of the year (is Kate Middleton an AI 🤔).

🍺 See you Friday, Bros 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros Town Hall, Be There or Be Square
entry.summary:
🧢 Hi Bros 🧢,
Join us tomorrow at the usual time for one of our Town Halls.
What is a Tech Bros Town Hall, you ask?
It's a time to hear Mary and Lauren rant about, well, their latest rants–which may or may not include:
• A discussion of where the newest trendy titles land on the "is this a B.S.-role?" scale, starting with "Field CTO." What does a Field CTO do? Are they truly.. outstanding in their field??
• Why does the New York Times hate Postgres??
• Will anyone admit that they were or maybe still are an Andrew Huberman fan? (For the record, Mary dislikes Andrew H. nearly as much as she loathes Lex F.)
• Did we find Kate Middleton whilst we were out standing in the field for this *definitely real and shot at the BBC Studios and not a deep fake at all* cover photo?
🍺 See you at the usual time, bros. 10am PT / 1pm ET / Happy Hour for bros everywhere 🍺
entry.content:
🧢 Hi Bros 🧢,

Join us tomorrow at the usual time for one of our Town Halls.

What is a Tech Bros Town Hall, you ask?

It's a time to hear Mary and Lauren rant about, well, their latest rants–which may or may not include:

• A discussion of where the newest trendy titles land on the "is this a B.S.-role?" scale, starting with "Field CTO." What does a Field CTO do? Are they truly.. outstanding in their field??

• Why does the New York Times hate Postgres??

• Will anyone admit that they were or maybe still are an Andrew Huberman fan? (For the record, Mary dislikes Andrew H. nearly as much as she loathes Lex F.)

• Did we find Kate Middleton whilst we were out standing in the field for this *definitely real and shot at the BBC Studios and not a deep fake at all* cover photo?

🍺 See you at the usual time, bros. 10am PT / 1pm ET / Happy Hour for bros everywhere 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros: Pivots, Progress & Price Inflation @Data Startups w/ Nick Freund
entry.summary:
🧢 Hey Bros 🧢
Join us on Friday, March 15 for a very special edition of the Tech Bros, with a rare occurrance - the return of a previous guest: Nicholas Freund of Workstream.io.
We first spoke to Nick during the collapse of Silicon Valley Bank, when he was one of the few founders willing to speak openly about the crisis as it unraveled.
This time around, he'll be telling us the nitty-gritty about what it was like to be running a data startup during the, in his words, "shitshow that was 2023."
He'll give us his take on: • why sales fell of a cliff • why pricing inflation in SaaS is killing data startups • why companies need to get real about PROVIDING VALUE before ASKING FOR VALUE.
Nick is known for his Silicon Alley not Silicon Valley astuteness on the market, and also for his candor and transparency... so click "Attend" and get your 🍿 and 🍻 and be ready to settle in for a must-watch episode 👏👏👏
entry.content:
🧢 Hey Bros 🧢

Join us on Friday, March 15 for a very special edition of the Tech Bros, with a rare occurrance - the return of a previous guest: Nicholas Freund of Workstream.io.

We first spoke to Nick during the collapse of Silicon Valley Bank, when he was one of the few founders willing to speak openly about the crisis as it unraveled.

This time around, he'll be telling us the nitty-gritty about what it was like to be running a data startup during the, in his words, "shitshow that was 2023."

He'll give us his take on:
• why sales fell of a cliff
• why pricing inflation in SaaS is killing data startups
• why companies need to get real about PROVIDING VALUE before ASKING FOR VALUE.

Nick is known for his Silicon Alley not Silicon Valley astuteness on the market, and also for his candor and transparency... so click "Attend" and get your 🍿 and 🍻 and be ready to settle in for a must-watch episode 👏👏👏

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros teach you how to negotiate layoffs, severance, getting fired–featuring Dan Goodman
entry.summary:
🧢 Hi Bros 🧢
Most of us know it all too well: it's still lay-off season in what seems to have become a permanent lay-off season for tech workers.
We know that lots of you have experienced job loss or are living in the fear of getting the axe... So this week we're bringing you a very special guest who has top-notch advice on how to handle negotiations when you lose your job.
Negotiations, you say?
You mean that as an employee, you have the right to negotiate?
You might actually have some power and agency???
💡💡💡 Yes–even in late stage unfettered capitalism, you have more rights than you think 💡💡💡
Dan Goodman has built an incredibly successful business as an employee advocate, teaching tech workers (and folks from other industries) how to make the most of these challenging situations...
Oftentimes, they go from zero severance to walking away with a check.
Always, they end up walking away with their dignity intact.
This is a must-see episode for any of you all who, like us, actually work for a living. Tell your friends, family, and anyone who's ever been treated unjustly by a greedy or ungrateful employer.
As always, we'll have plenty of time for questions.
🍻 See you at the usual time: 10am PT, 1pm ET, Happy Hour everywhere🍻
entry.content:
🧢 Hi Bros 🧢

Most of us know it all too well: it's still lay-off season in what seems to have become a permanent lay-off season for tech workers.

We know that lots of you have experienced job loss or are living in the fear of getting the axe... So this week we're bringing you a very special guest who has top-notch advice on how to handle negotiations when you lose your job.

Negotiations, you say?

You mean that as an employee, you have the right to negotiate?

You might actually have some power and agency???

💡💡💡 Yes–even in late stage unfettered capitalism, you have more rights than you think 💡💡💡

Dan Goodman has built an incredibly successful business as an employee advocate, teaching tech workers (and folks from other industries) how to make the most of these challenging situations...

Oftentimes, they go from zero severance to walking away with a check.

Always, they end up walking away with their dignity intact.

This is a must-see episode for any of you all who, like us, actually work for a living. Tell your friends, family, and anyone who's ever been treated unjustly by a greedy or ungrateful employer.

As always, we'll have plenty of time for questions.

🍻 See you at the usual time: 10am PT, 1pm ET, Happy Hour everywhere🍻

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros Talk Product Orgs, w/ Deb Roy Chowdhury
entry.summary:
🧢 Coming up on the Tech Bros this week: we’re sitting down with Deb Roy Chowdhury of InfinityOn to learn more about running Product orgs at data companies.
Deb is an expert on streaming and database management, and he’s got a lot of great opinions on what works and what doesn’t in 2024.
Lauren will even be around as she takes some time off from hunting down CAVA health grades and CAVA health standards.
Join us at 10am PT / 1pm ET / Happy Hour everywhere 🍺
entry.content:
🧢 Coming up on the Tech Bros this week: we’re sitting down with Deb Roy Chowdhury of InfinityOn to learn more about running Product orgs at data companies.

Deb is an expert on streaming and database management, and he’s got a lot of great opinions on what works and what doesn’t in 2024.

Lauren will even be around as she takes some time off from hunting down CAVA health grades and CAVA health standards.

Join us at 10am PT / 1pm ET / Happy Hour everywhere 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros Take on 2024
entry.summary:
Love us or hate us, the Tech Bros are back!
We've received many missives from concerned viewers wondering if perhaps the Tech Bros had hung up their 🧢 hats 🧢 at the end of 2023.
Fear not, dear friends, Lauren and I are back in the saddle and, dare we say, better than ever.
🤠🤠🤠
Let's do a Town Hall this week – we want to hear from you and, most importantly, hear WHAT YOU WANT FROM US as we head into another insane year of hype cycles, influencer and mascot nonsense, & startups on the fast track to nowhere.
🍺 Join us here live at our usual time: Friday 10am ET / 1 pm PT / Happy Hour Everywhere 🍺
entry.content:
Love us or hate us, the Tech Bros are back!

We've received many missives from concerned viewers wondering if perhaps the Tech Bros had hung up their 🧢 hats 🧢 at the end of 2023.

Fear not, dear friends, Lauren and I are back in the saddle and, dare we say, better than ever.

🤠🤠🤠

Let's do a Town Hall this week – we want to hear from you and, most importantly, hear WHAT YOU WANT FROM US as we head into another insane year of hype cycles, influencer and mascot nonsense, & startups on the fast track to nowhere.

🍺 Join us here live at our usual time: Friday 10am ET / 1 pm PT / Happy Hour Everywhere 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros talk to Soren Larson of Crosshatch
entry.summary:
🧢 Hey Bros 🧢
Join us 1pm PT / 4pm ET tomorrow for a rare THURSDAY edition of the Tech Bros Show.
This week Lauren and I chat with Soren Larson, CEO & Founder of Crosshatch.
We’ll get Soren’s takes on: • The state of data collection today • What matters, and what we all should know, about personalization and privacy • Anything else y’all want to talk about!
🍻 See you then!! 🍻
entry.content:
🧢 Hey Bros 🧢

Join us 1pm PT / 4pm ET tomorrow for a rare THURSDAY edition of the Tech Bros Show.

This week Lauren and I chat with Soren Larson, CEO & Founder of Crosshatch.

We’ll get Soren’s takes on:
• The state of data collection today
• What matters, and what we all should know, about personalization and privacy
• Anything else y’all want to talk about!

🍻 See you then!! 🍻

entry.title: [TECHBROS] TAKE 2: Tech Bros talk M & A & more w/ Raj Sen
entry.summary:
🧢 Hey Bros 🧢
This week we're bringing you the long-awaited conversation with Rajkumar Sen, Co-Founder & CTO of Arcion Labs, who recently sold his company to Databricks.
We'll get into:
• What’s next on the horizon for Raj and the Databricks team?
• What does this mean for the rest of the space?
• What's it like to sell the company you’ve worked on for several years?
As always, bring your questions live!
🧢 See you tomorrow (Friday) at our usual time: 10am PT / 1pm ET 🧢
entry.content:
🧢 Hey Bros 🧢

This week we're bringing you the long-awaited conversation with Rajkumar Sen, Co-Founder & CTO of Arcion Labs, who recently sold his company to Databricks.

We'll get into:

• What’s next on the horizon for Raj and the Databricks team?

• What does this mean for the rest of the space?

• What's it like to sell the company you’ve worked on for several years?

As always, bring your questions live!

🧢 See you tomorrow (Friday) at our usual time: 10am PT / 1pm ET 🧢

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros Talk Events Data w/ Timo Dechau
entry.summary:
🧢 Tune in tomorrow for a Tech Bros Show with one of our greatest Tech Bro bros, Timo Dechau 🧢
We'll be talking events data: what's new, what you need to know, and should it really be called events data anyway?
Timo will also tell us about his forthcoming book.
As always, Lauren and I will bring our usual complaints about all kinds of nonsense happening in data and tech.
And viewers can join in, in the comments or live on screen, Town Hall style.
🍺 Tune in 10am PT / 1pm ET / Happy Hour everywhere 🍺
entry.content:
🧢 Tune in tomorrow for a Tech Bros Show with one of our greatest Tech Bro bros, Timo Dechau 🧢

We'll be talking events data: what's new, what you need to know, and should it really be called events data anyway?

Timo will also tell us about his forthcoming book.

As always, Lauren and I will bring our usual complaints about all kinds of nonsense happening in data and tech.

And viewers can join in, in the comments or live on screen, Town Hall style.

🍺 Tune in 10am PT / 1pm ET / Happy Hour everywhere 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] CDPs & Wannabes: Tech Bros featuring Arpit from Databeats
entry.summary:
🧢 Hey Bros 🧢
Get ready for another fantastic bro-ing out session with one of our favorite voices on data: Arpit Choudhury.
Arpit is the founder of Databeats and, as his many followers, know he's one of the most informed voices on Customer Data Platforms and related tools...
In true Tech Bro style, he doesn't hesitate to call out marketing BS and we can't wait to hear what's annoying him the most these days 😂
We'll also get a 🌎 WORLD-EXCLUSIVE sneak peek 🌎 into his upcoming book called Good Data to Growth.
🍺Join us at our usual time: 9am PT / noon ET / Friday Happy Hour everywhere 🍺
entry.content:
🧢 Hey Bros 🧢

Get ready for another fantastic bro-ing out session with one of our favorite voices on data: Arpit Choudhury.

Arpit is the founder of Databeats and, as his many followers, know he's one of the most informed voices on Customer Data Platforms and related tools...

In true Tech Bro style, he doesn't hesitate to call out marketing BS and we can't wait to hear what's annoying him the most these days 😂

We'll also get a 🌎 WORLD-EXCLUSIVE sneak peek 🌎 into his upcoming book called Good Data to Growth.

🍺Join us at our usual time: 9am PT / noon ET / Friday Happy Hour everywhere 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros Town Hall: Open Forum
entry.summary:
There's lots going on in the tech world.
Tesla...had a bad quarter? The Gitex Tech Conference in Dubai welcomed 170k attendees. The job market is still...stagnant? Where's Chat-GPT at these days? That seems quiet. Lauren is making all the Composable CDP vendors fight...again.
We're having one of our open forums this Friday, Town Hall style.
Have something to say/vent? Want to ask questions? Hop on on.
Friday, 1pm Eastern, 10am Pacific
entry.content:
There's lots going on in the tech world.

Tesla...had a bad quarter? The Gitex Tech Conference in Dubai welcomed 170k attendees. The job market is still...stagnant? Where's Chat-GPT at these days? That seems quiet. Lauren is making all the Composable CDP vendors fight...again.

We're having one of our open forums this Friday, Town Hall style.

Have something to say/vent? Want to ask questions? Hop on on.

Friday, 1pm Eastern, 10am Pacific

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros Take on Legacy Tech
entry.summary:
What is 'legacy' tech anyway???
In honor of Lauren becoming one year older without any significant updates or new releases this year, the Tech Bros sit down with guest Phillip Hall to talk legacy vs. cool, shiny, new tech.
We'll have plenty of party games, and discuss what is and is not legacy and how businesses should approach switching from legacy solutions.
entry.content:
What is 'legacy' tech anyway???

In honor of Lauren becoming one year older without any significant updates or new releases this year, the Tech Bros sit down with guest Phillip Hall to talk legacy vs. cool, shiny, new tech.

We'll have plenty of party games, and discuss what is and is not legacy and how businesses should approach switching from legacy solutions.

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros Talk Batch vs. Streaming, w/ John Kutay
entry.summary:
🧢 Hi Bros 🧢
Everyone knows John Kutay.
He's: -Amazing at glancing into the middle distance
-Great at pairing denim
-Expert in explaining why and how batch systems fail.
In tomorrow's show, we sit down with John - who's the Director of Product at Striim - to go deep on why too many organizations are failing with batch at scale, and how they should be thinking about approaching streaming data.
Come learn with us, and as always bring your questions, compliments, concerns, and rants!
11am PT / 2pm ET
🍺 See you then, Bros 🍺
entry.content:
🧢 Hi Bros 🧢

Everyone knows John Kutay.

He's:
-Amazing at glancing into the middle distance

-Great at pairing denim

-Expert in explaining why and how batch systems fail.

In tomorrow's show, we sit down with John - who's the Director of Product at Striim - to go deep on why too many organizations are failing with batch at scale, and how they should be thinking about approaching streaming data.

Come learn with us, and as always bring your questions, compliments, concerns, and rants!

11am PT / 2pm ET

🍺 See you then, Bros 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros Take 2: Taking on BI, w/ Bethany
entry.summary:
This week, Lauren and I will bring you our long-awaited interview with Bethany Lyons, diving into BI...
(Yes, this is a Take 2: We'd scheduled this a few weeks ago, but Mary mixed up the times 🙃)
Bethany will bring all her hot takes on the data industry... including some myth-busting about what exactly is going on in the Business Intelligence world.
On the agenda: - Do you need three BI tools at your company? - How should organizations be thinking about compute and BI? - Since every vendor likes calling everything else dead, we must ask: Is BI Dead? Is the Dashboard Dead?
🧢 See you all tomorrow (Friday) at 9am PT / noon ET, Bros 🧢
entry.content:
This week, Lauren and I will bring you our long-awaited interview with Bethany Lyons, diving into BI...

(Yes, this is a Take 2: We'd scheduled this a few weeks ago, but Mary mixed up the times 🙃)

Bethany will bring all her hot takes on the data industry... including some myth-busting about what exactly is going on in the Business Intelligence world.

On the agenda:
- Do you need three BI tools at your company?
- How should organizations be thinking about compute and BI?
- Since every vendor likes calling everything else dead, we must ask: Is BI Dead? Is the Dashboard Dead?

🧢 See you all tomorrow (Friday) at 9am PT / noon ET, Bros 🧢

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros: Town Hall Classic Edition
entry.summary:
🧢 The Tech Bros have had a lot going on 🧢
Lauren has blown up on Twitter, and her investigative skills have been making waves not just in data and tech but also in media and finance.
Mary went viral for reasons altogether unrelated to tech.
We've missed you and we're back tomorrow.
No guests, so we'll interview each other. And y'all are welcome to join us, in the comments and/or onscreen.
We'll also give you a sneak peek into some of the great guests we have lined up for the fall.
🍺 10am PT / 1pm ET / Happy Hour Everywhere 🍺
entry.content:
🧢 The Tech Bros have had a lot going on 🧢

Lauren has blown up on Twitter, and her investigative skills have been making waves not just in data and tech but also in media and finance.

Mary went viral for reasons altogether unrelated to tech.

We've missed you and we're back tomorrow.

No guests, so we'll interview each other. And y'all are welcome to join us, in the comments and/or onscreen.

We'll also give you a sneak peek into some of the great guests we have lined up for the fall.

🍺 10am PT / 1pm ET / Happy Hour Everywhere 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] 🧢 The Tech Bros talk How to be a Data Consultant in 2023 🧢
entry.summary:
Hi Bros,
Starting one's own consulting firm is an option that lots of data and tech people are pursuing these days.
Sometimes they're motivated by a lay-off and the need to find paid work.
More often, from what I've seen, they're motivated by a deeply-held desire to be one's own boss.
But–where do you start?
How do you land your first customers and retain them over time?
Do you cast a wide net or focus on a specific niche?
Lauren and I both have experience running a consultancy (I've done it in the past, and she's currently running a wildly successful one).
But we'll also be joined by two of our favorite people from the data world, who'll bring their own advice, and 🌶️ strong opinions 🌶️: Joe Naso and Ricky Martin.
🍺 Join us at the usual time: 11am PT/ 2pm ET/Happy Hour everywhere. See you then, Bros! 🍺
entry.content:
Hi Bros,

Starting one's own consulting firm is an option that lots of data and tech people are pursuing these days.

Sometimes they're motivated by a lay-off and the need to find paid work.

More often, from what I've seen, they're motivated by a deeply-held desire to be one's own boss.

But–where do you start?

How do you land your first customers and retain them over time?

Do you cast a wide net or focus on a specific niche?

Lauren and I both have experience running a consultancy (I've done it in the past, and she's currently running a wildly successful one).

But we'll also be joined by two of our favorite people from the data world, who'll bring their own advice, and 🌶️ strong opinions 🌶️: Joe Naso and Ricky Martin.

🍺 Join us at the usual time: 11am PT/ 2pm ET/Happy Hour everywhere. See you then, Bros! 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros Emergency Town Hall Meeting: Data Teams Running Accounting & Finance Processes
entry.summary:
Hi Bros,
It's almost Friday, and hold your lovely Tech Bro 🧢 hats 🧢 because this week's show is going to be a good one...
We're taking a close look at a pattern that many of us have seen thrown around more and more: the usage of data teams and data tools for doing accounting and finance work in organizations.
This can range from a data engineer using SQL in a cloud data warehouse to manage accounting logic, to using BI and reporting tools to serve data dumps for Quote-to-Cash processes... And everything in between.
But–why are data teams doing this? More importantly, *should* data teams be doing this?
If so, where is the line and what tools should be used?
If not, how can data teams push back or manage expectations?
Does any of this change if we are talking about public or regulated companies vs. run-of-the-mill tech startups?
We want to hear from you, so the show will be Town Hall format... with special guest Ethan Aaron who has 🌶️ just a few 🌶️ opinions on this.
Join us just slightly later than our usual time: 🍺 noon PT / 3pm / still Happy Hour everywhere 🍺
entry.content:
Hi Bros,

It's almost Friday, and hold your lovely Tech Bro 🧢 hats 🧢 because this week's show is going to be a good one...

We're taking a close look at a pattern that many of us have seen thrown around more and more: the usage of data teams and data tools for doing accounting and finance work in organizations.

This can range from a data engineer using SQL in a cloud data warehouse to manage accounting logic, to using BI and reporting tools to serve data dumps for Quote-to-Cash processes... And everything in between.

But–why are data teams doing this? More importantly, *should* data teams be doing this?

If so, where is the line and what tools should be used?

If not, how can data teams push back or manage expectations?

Does any of this change if we are talking about public or regulated companies vs. run-of-the-mill tech startups?

We want to hear from you, so the show will be Town Hall format... with special guest Ethan Aaron who has 🌶️ just a few 🌶️ opinions on this.

Join us just slightly later than our usual time: 🍺 noon PT / 3pm / still Happy Hour everywhere 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros talk Customer Success: featuring Rachel Herrera
entry.summary:
On tomorrow's Tech Bros Show, we dive into a topic that's quite near and dear to our hearts: Customer Success.
Why is this not-so-flashy topic top of mind for us?
Well, Lauren's fans know that she's big on customer empathy. She doesn't hold back from calling out companies that don't *actually care* about their customers. And for the businesses that DO CARE, having a top-notch Customer Success team can be as important as your product itself - when it comes to delivering on the promises you've made.
My interest in Customer Success comes from a different angle. I love digging into the less-talked-about roles in tech. How does one get into Customer Success, and how do they move forward to build a career in this niche that's kinda overlooked? What types of individuals excel in these roles?
Joining us: Rachel Herrera, current Customer Success Lead at Kubit (with earlier experiences at Amplitude and Slalom). She'll answer all our questions, and yours, about Customer Success... and will also give us her hot takes on tech/data/customer success/whatever y'all want to ask her.
(Full disclosure: yes Rachel and I currently work together. It's my show, I can invite who I want.)
Join us at the usual time: 10am PT/1pm ET/Happy Hour everywhere!!
entry.content:
On tomorrow's Tech Bros Show, we dive into a topic that's quite near and dear to our hearts: Customer Success.

Why is this not-so-flashy topic top of mind for us?

Well, Lauren's fans know that she's big on customer empathy. She doesn't hold back from calling out companies that don't *actually care* about their customers. And for the businesses that DO CARE, having a top-notch Customer Success team can be as important as your product itself - when it comes to delivering on the promises you've made.

My interest in Customer Success comes from a different angle. I love digging into the less-talked-about roles in tech. How does one get into Customer Success, and how do they move forward to build a career in this niche that's kinda overlooked? What types of individuals excel in these roles?

Joining us: Rachel Herrera, current Customer Success Lead at Kubit (with earlier experiences at Amplitude and Slalom). She'll answer all our questions, and yours, about Customer Success... and will also give us her hot takes on tech/data/customer success/whatever y'all want to ask her.

(Full disclosure: yes Rachel and I currently work together. It's my show, I can invite who I want.)

Join us at the usual time: 10am PT/1pm ET/Happy Hour everywhere!!

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros sit down with Wiley author Vin Vashishta
entry.summary:
In this week's Tech Bros Show, we sit down with one of the data experts, dare we say data influencers, who we've long appreciated for his pragmatic, business-minded, and no-bs perspective: the founder of V Squared consulting, Vin Vashishta.
In fact, Vin just packaged his views into a Wiley book: From Data to Profit, How Businesses Leverage Data to Grow Their Top and Bottom Lines.
Join us at the usual Friday time (10am PT/1pm ET/Happy Hour Everywhere), and bring your own questions for Vin. We'll be asking him: - As someone who's frank about the fact that many well-run businesses still fail to be data-driven, how can he be enthusiastic about AI? - Is the AI hype cycle different than the data science hype cycle, and if so, how? - What are his predictions RE how Google will pivot in an AI-led world? - From his work as a consultant, what's missing from the "thought leadership" discussions about data these days? - What's his take on how those of us who work in data (and tech at large) can be on the right side of history these days? (Other than, you know, coming on the Tech Bros Show!)
See you soon, Bros!!
entry.content:
In this week's Tech Bros Show, we sit down with one of the data experts, dare we say data influencers, who we've long appreciated for his pragmatic, business-minded, and no-bs perspective: the founder of V Squared consulting, Vin Vashishta.

In fact, Vin just packaged his views into a Wiley book: From Data to Profit, How Businesses Leverage Data to Grow Their Top and Bottom Lines.

Join us at the usual Friday time (10am PT/1pm ET/Happy Hour Everywhere), and bring your own questions for Vin. We'll be asking him:
- As someone who's frank about the fact that many well-run businesses still fail to be data-driven, how can he be enthusiastic about AI?
- Is the AI hype cycle different than the data science hype cycle, and if so, how?
- What are his predictions RE how Google will pivot in an AI-led world?
- From his work as a consultant, what's missing from the "thought leadership" discussions about data these days?
- What's his take on how those of us who work in data (and tech at large) can be on the right side of history these days? (Other than, you know, coming on the Tech Bros Show!)

See you soon, Bros!!

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros Show is back with a Friday 🎉 Summer Party Extravaganza Fest 🎉episode
entry.summary:
First of all, our heartfelt apologies for depriving everyone from the Tech Bros Show the past couple of weeks–we’ve both been busy with work travel.
We promise to make it up to you with the first official SUMMER TECH BROS party.
Join us tomorrow at our usual Friday time (10am PT/1pm ET/Happy Hour everywhere).
We’ll be joined by Petrica Leuca who’ll bring her hot takes on many hot topics, including: • How we used to do analytics before *size mattered* • Data contracts • Reverse ETL • More broadly, her love-hate relationship with the Modern Data Stack… from how fast it can be to do data experimentation, to the sometimes overwhelming complexity of integrating tools
Petrica is a true Tech Bro, that’s to say, the real deal - a data engineer with 15+ years of experience across many industries, and a blogger on Medium.
See you tomorrow, Bros! Mary & Lauren
entry.content:
First of all, our heartfelt apologies for depriving everyone from the Tech Bros Show the past couple of weeks–we’ve both been busy with work travel.

We promise to make it up to you with the first official SUMMER TECH BROS party.

Join us tomorrow at our usual Friday time (10am PT/1pm ET/Happy Hour everywhere).

We’ll be joined by Petrica Leuca who’ll bring her hot takes on many hot topics, including:
• How we used to do analytics before *size mattered*
• Data contracts
• Reverse ETL
• More broadly, her love-hate relationship with the Modern Data Stack… from how fast it can be to do data experimentation, to the sometimes overwhelming complexity of integrating tools

Petrica is a true Tech Bro, that’s to say, the real deal - a data engineer with 15+ years of experience across many industries, and a blogger on Medium.

See you tomorrow, Bros!
Mary & Lauren

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros talk Data Strategy & Leadership: featuring Aaron Wilkerson of Carhartt
entry.summary:
The Tech Bros Show is back this week, and we're featuring Aaron Wilkerson - a Senior Manager of Data Governance & Strategy at Carhartt.
We've been wanting to get Aaron on the show for awhile, after discovering his wise posts about data strategy, leadership, and governance right here on LinkedIn.
Among the topics we'll cover: - What does it mean to be a data leader? - Does data governance mean the same thing in all organizations? - Are data people sufficiently strategic? (From Mary's perspective, the answer is no!) - Is the data community (whatever that means) too inward-facing? - Since we're in a busy events season for us data folk, what's Aaron's take on events - and how can attendees get the most from what can sometimes be - let's face-it - a rather tedious and trying experience?
We'll also ask whatever Aaron can share about working at Carhartt (a legendary company).
As usual, we'll open the second half of the show to viewers who want to join live with their questions for Aaron or us!
🍺 See you then, Bros! Oh and it's the usual time - 10am PT/1pm ET. 🍺
entry.content:
The Tech Bros Show is back this week, and we're featuring Aaron Wilkerson - a Senior Manager of Data Governance & Strategy at Carhartt.

We've been wanting to get Aaron on the show for awhile, after discovering his wise posts about data strategy, leadership, and governance right here on LinkedIn.

Among the topics we'll cover:
- What does it mean to be a data leader?
- Does data governance mean the same thing in all organizations?
- Are data people sufficiently strategic? (From Mary's perspective, the answer is no!)
- Is the data community (whatever that means) too inward-facing?
- Since we're in a busy events season for us data folk, what's Aaron's take on events - and how can attendees get the most from what can sometimes be - let's face-it - a rather tedious and trying experience?

We'll also ask whatever Aaron can share about working at Carhartt (a legendary company).

As usual, we'll open the second half of the show to viewers who want to join live with their questions for Aaron or us!

🍺 See you then, Bros! Oh and it's the usual time - 10am PT/1pm ET. 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros are MOVING PAST DEBEZIUM
entry.summary:
We may be The Tech Bros but actually deep down we're Data Bros, and - as you all know - sometimes we go into the weeds on data stuff.
This is gonna be one of those weeks.
We'll be talking DEBEZIUM.
Debezium has been the Change Data Capture standard the last few years, but what does Debezium get wrong?
What are companies getting wrong with ELT vs. ETL today?
What should change with Airflow and dbt transformation patterns?
What’s all the hate around CDC, when most batch ingest vendors do CDC anyway and then delay ingest for customers?
Yes, we're in for a technical deep dive.
Bring your questions, we’ll have great answers with two top technical architects: Johnny Graettinger of Estuary and Rajkumar Sen of Arcion Labs.
Find us at the usual Silicon Valley Happy Hour time - that's 10am PT / 1pm ET - live on Linkedin and over on YouTube as well.
See you there, Bros.
entry.content:
We may be The Tech Bros but actually deep down we're Data Bros, and - as you all know - sometimes we go into the weeds on data stuff.

This is gonna be one of those weeks.

We'll be talking DEBEZIUM.

Debezium has been the Change Data Capture standard the last few years, but what does Debezium get wrong?

What are companies getting wrong with ELT vs. ETL today?

What should change with Airflow and dbt transformation patterns?

What’s all the hate around CDC, when most batch ingest vendors do CDC anyway and then delay ingest for customers?

Yes, we're in for a technical deep dive.

Bring your questions, we’ll have great answers with two top technical architects: Johnny Graettinger of Estuary and Rajkumar Sen of Arcion Labs.

Find us at the usual Silicon Valley Happy Hour time - that's 10am PT / 1pm ET - live on Linkedin and over on YouTube as well.

See you there, Bros.

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros Talk LLMs and NLPs: featuring Chris Brousseau
entry.summary:
Hi Bros,
This week we go deep on LLMs and NLP with Chris Brousseau, a data scientist at Mastercard who'll tell us about:
- his work on NLP and LLMs, in his current role as a data scientist at Mastercard, as well as at a few other companies - his thoughts on the LLM and NLP hype in a ChatGPT world–how much is just noise, and what should we really be focused on / be learning / be ignoring /be afraid of? - where does he think LLMs are going next in terms of big innovations?
We'll also hear about the book that Chris is co-authoring, about LLMs in production.
And we'll ask for tips on the best way to get started in NLP and LLMS. Chris has a pretty fascinating education and career path that started with computational linguistics and includes working as a Russian and Slovak translator.
As is now our tradition, we'll open up the show for viewers who'd like to join us live, Town Hall style, to ask Chris (or us) any questions and to share their own brilliant commentary.
🍺 See you tomorrow, Bros! Silicon Valley Happy Hour time - that's 10am PT, 1pm ET 🍺
entry.content:
Hi Bros,

This week we go deep on LLMs and NLP with Chris Brousseau, a data scientist at Mastercard who'll tell us about:

- his work on NLP and LLMs, in his current role as a data scientist at Mastercard, as well as at a few other companies
- his thoughts on the LLM and NLP hype in a ChatGPT world–how much is just noise, and what should we really be focused on / be learning / be ignoring /be afraid of?
- where does he think LLMs are going next in terms of big innovations?

We'll also hear about the book that Chris is co-authoring, about LLMs in production.

And we'll ask for tips on the best way to get started in NLP and LLMS. Chris has a pretty fascinating education and career path that started with computational linguistics and includes working as a Russian and Slovak translator.

As is now our tradition, we'll open up the show for viewers who'd like to join us live, Town Hall style, to ask Chris (or us) any questions and to share their own brilliant commentary.

🍺 See you tomorrow, Bros! Silicon Valley Happy Hour time - that's 10am PT, 1pm ET 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros talk Data Scams: w/ Bill Inmon
entry.summary:
At the Tech Bros Show pride ourselves on offering unabashed critiques of anything and everything happening in #tech and #data, and this Friday's show will not disappoint.
Lauren and I will be joined by our buddy, the one-and-only Bill Inmon, to discuss some of the many SCAMS out there in the data world:
• As a buyer, what should you watch out for when evaluating vendor claims?
• How can you tell if a product is vaporware?
• Is free open source ever really free? And how can data professionals evaluate the TCO of free, open source software?
• Where should data professionals get guidance, and how much should they trust industry analysts and VCs?
As usual, we'll open up the second half of the show to viewers who'd like to join us on camera to share their own questions, experiences, and rants!
🍺 Friday 10am PT / 1pm ET - see you there, Bros! 🍺
entry.content:
At the Tech Bros Show pride ourselves on offering unabashed critiques of anything and everything happening in #tech and #data, and this Friday's show will not disappoint.

Lauren and I will be joined by our buddy, the one-and-only Bill Inmon, to discuss some of the many SCAMS out there in the data world:

• As a buyer, what should you watch out for when evaluating vendor claims?

• How can you tell if a product is vaporware?

• Is free open source ever really free? And how can data professionals evaluate the TCO of free, open source software?

• Where should data professionals get guidance, and how much should they trust industry analysts and VCs?

As usual, we'll open up the second half of the show to viewers who'd like to join us on camera to share their own questions, experiences, and rants!

🍺 Friday 10am PT / 1pm ET - see you there, Bros! 🍺

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros talk Data Elephants in The Room
entry.summary:
Hi Bros,
Tomorrow we'll be having a long-overdue chat about some 🐘 elephants 🐘 in the room, in the data world...
We'll be featuring one of our favorite Tech Bro CEOs, Sarah McKenna of Sequentum, who has a lot to say about the aforementioned elephants–in particular:
👀 With the rise in data tooling, is anyone managing risk? Is there enough awareness of the multiple business risks inherent to these tools and workflows? WHERE ARE THE ADULTS?
🔭 Can we just "shift left" data quality and observability? What does data observability even mean, anyway?
🌐 With the rise of ChatGPT, is web scraping more important than ever?
Keeping up a now three-week old Tech Bros Show tradition, we'll open the second half of the show as a Town Hall for anyone who'd like to join us live on the show–with their questions and comments for us and especially for our guest Sarah.
See y'all at the usual Friday Silicon Valley & Silicon Alley 🍺 Happy Hour🍺 time - that's 10am PT / 1pm ET.
entry.content:
Hi Bros,

Tomorrow we'll be having a long-overdue chat about some 🐘 elephants 🐘 in the room, in the data world...

We'll be featuring one of our favorite Tech Bro CEOs, Sarah McKenna of Sequentum, who has a lot to say about the aforementioned elephants–in particular:

👀 With the rise in data tooling, is anyone managing risk? Is there enough awareness of the multiple business risks inherent to these tools and workflows? WHERE ARE THE ADULTS?

🔭 Can we just "shift left" data quality and observability? What does data observability even mean, anyway?

🌐 With the rise of ChatGPT, is web scraping more important than ever?

Keeping up a now three-week old Tech Bros Show tradition, we'll open the second half of the show as a Town Hall for anyone who'd like to join us live on the show–with their questions and comments for us and especially for our guest Sarah.

See y'all at the usual Friday Silicon Valley & Silicon Alley 🍺 Happy Hour🍺 time - that's 10am PT / 1pm ET.

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros & Super Data Brothers 🇺🇸 Town Hall 🇺🇸
entry.summary:
Join us this Friday (omg that's tomorrow, where did the week go?) for one of the most Special of Special Editions of the The Tech Bros Show.
Why is it Special, you ask?
Two reasons.
FIRST: We'll be joined by the hosts of another top tech show, the Super Data Brothers Ryan Dolley and Eric Dolley.
Ryan and Eric are so much Bro-ier than Lauren and I could ever hope to be that we might just hand the reins over to them and 🍺 kick back 🍺 to hear their rants on everything from LLMs to the latest conferences they've attended to the realities of the job market right now.
SECOND: Based on the success of our All Hands/Town Hall/interactive format of last week's show, we'll once again be inviting viewers to join us live with their questions, concerns, complaints, and 🔮 prophecies🔮
We'll post the Streamyard link in the comments and look forward to seeing many of your beautiful bro faces in our virtual studio ❤️❤️ ❤️
See y'all at close to the usual time: 11am PT/2pm ET/International Happy Hour.
#data #tech #womenintech #dataengineering #analytics
entry.content:
Join us this Friday (omg that's tomorrow, where did the week go?) for one of the most Special of Special Editions of the The Tech Bros Show.

Why is it Special, you ask?

Two reasons.

FIRST: We'll be joined by the hosts of another top tech show, the Super Data Brothers Ryan Dolley and Eric Dolley.

Ryan and Eric are so much Bro-ier than Lauren and I could ever hope to be that we might just hand the reins over to them and 🍺 kick back 🍺 to hear their rants on everything from LLMs to the latest conferences they've attended to the realities of the job market right now.

SECOND: Based on the success of our All Hands/Town Hall/interactive format of last week's show, we'll once again be inviting viewers to join us live with their questions, concerns, complaints, and 🔮 prophecies🔮

We'll post the Streamyard link in the comments and look forward to seeing many of your beautiful bro faces in our virtual studio ❤️❤️ ❤️

See y'all at close to the usual time: 11am PT/2pm ET/International Happy Hour.

#data #tech #womenintech #dataengineering #analytics

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tune in for the first official TECH BROS ALL HANDS
entry.summary:
Hi friends,
As the leaders of a now long-established Establishment in the Tech World, dare we say a veritable Institution, the hosts of the Tech Bros Show thought it was only fitting that we invite all of you to an All Hands meeting...
A friendly Friday morning gathering at which your Beloved Hosts can more clearly present our own Agenda...
And, most importantly, an opportunity for all of you to voice very own Tech Bro opinions, in the spirit of transparency and good will that our esteemed industry is known for.
Tune in at the usual time (10am PT / 1pm ET / or "Happy Hour" as Lauren calls it 🍺).
Viewers will be invited to JOIN US LIVE to ask questions - not just in the comments but ON CAMERA. We'll put the Streamyard link in the comments and we're confident that our live-streaming technology will work better than Netflix's.
We're looking forward to SEEING your beautiful faces and HEARING your beautiful voices, Bros.
#tech #techbros #womenintech #data #dataengineering #softwareengineering
entry.content:
Hi friends,

As the leaders of a now long-established Establishment in the Tech World, dare we say a veritable Institution, the hosts of the Tech Bros Show thought it was only fitting that we invite all of you to an All Hands meeting...

A friendly Friday morning gathering at which your Beloved Hosts can more clearly present our own Agenda...

And, most importantly, an opportunity for all of you to voice very own Tech Bro opinions, in the spirit of transparency and good will that our esteemed industry is known for.

Tune in at the usual time (10am PT / 1pm ET / or "Happy Hour" as Lauren calls it 🍺).

Viewers will be invited to JOIN US LIVE to ask questions - not just in the comments but ON CAMERA. We'll put the Streamyard link in the comments and we're confident that our live-streaming technology will work better than Netflix's.

We're looking forward to SEEING your beautiful faces and HEARING your beautiful voices, Bros.

#tech #techbros #womenintech #data #dataengineering #softwareengineering

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros talk to a Top AI Bro: featuring Irene Bratsis, author of the AI Product Manager's Handbook
entry.summary:
Hi friends,
I trust that most of you survived a week without the Tech Bros.
Rest assured, we're back tomorrow, and this week we'll be joined by the uber-talented Tech Bro @Irene Bratsis – who's going to tell us all about her recently-published tome, the AI Product Manager's Handbook.
We'll ask Irene:
- How does it feel to have had the foresight to publish a major book on #AI exactly when the #AI hype cycle was about to take off? Talk about good instincts 👏👏👏
- For folks who haven't read her book yet, Irene will give us the highlights, and tell us who the book is intended for (Mary's take - it's not just for product managers – it's for anyone who wants non-jargon-y explanations of the data science and computer science behind AI).
- This is her first book – what was the hardest part of the book-writing process? What was the most fun? Did she actually have ChatGPT write the book for her?
🍺 We'l be live at our usual early Friday Happy Hour time: 10am PT / 1pm ET 🍺
Posting the Table of Contents below so y'all can do your homework and prep some questions for Irene 🤓
And huge thank you to our previous guest, Tech Bro @Michelle Yi, for using her #AI art skills to design our new logo – please chime in and let us know what you think!
entry.content:
Hi friends,

I trust that most of you survived a week without the Tech Bros.

Rest assured, we're back tomorrow, and this week we'll be joined by the uber-talented Tech Bro @Irene Bratsis – who's going to tell us all about her recently-published tome, the AI Product Manager's Handbook.

We'll ask Irene:

- How does it feel to have had the foresight to publish a major book on #AI exactly when the #AI hype cycle was about to take off? Talk about good instincts 👏👏👏

- For folks who haven't read her book yet, Irene will give us the highlights, and tell us who the book is intended for (Mary's take - it's not just for product managers – it's for anyone who wants non-jargon-y explanations of the data science and computer science behind AI).

- This is her first book – what was the hardest part of the book-writing process? What was the most fun? Did she actually have ChatGPT write the book for her?

🍺 We'l be live at our usual early Friday Happy Hour time: 10am PT / 1pm ET 🍺

Posting the Table of Contents below so y'all can do your homework and prep some questions for Irene 🤓

And huge thank you to our previous guest, Tech Bro @Michelle Yi, for using her #AI art skills to design our new logo – please chime in and let us know what you think!

entry.title: [TECHBROS] Tech Bros talk Data-for-Good: is it mostly a bunch of BS?
entry.summary:
Meta has one.
Bloomberg has one.
Harvard has one.
Your local library might have one, and your 6th-grader too.
What are we talking about?
"Data for Good" initiatives, or sometimes even entire institutions, which aim to leverage data to improve the world.
But - have any of these projects ever accomplished anything concrete?
Are they sometimes, maybe, perhaps - just PR stunts?
We'll talk all that and more with the renowned Data & AI leader Michelle Yi, who – among her CSV-receipt-long list of accomplishments - has founded and run Data-for-Good initiatives.
She'll bring us an insider's perspective on the Data for Good world, INCLUDING a healthy Tech Bro dose of SKEPTICISM.
Join us a bit later than usual this week - Tech Bros are gonna sleep in this Friday - we'll be going live at 4pm PT / 7pm ET.
🍺 Lauren promises to keep the drinking to a minimum even though its officially a Happy Hour show 🍿
🧢 See you then, Bros 🧢
entry.content:
Meta has one.

Bloomberg has one.

Harvard has one.

Your local library might have one, and your 6th-grader too.

What are we talking about?

"Data for Good" initiatives, or sometimes even entire institutions, which aim to leverage data to improve the world.

But - have any of these projects ever accomplished anything concrete?

Are they sometimes, maybe, perhaps - just PR stunts?

We'll talk all that and more with the renowned Data & AI leader Michelle Yi, who – among her CSV-receipt-long list of accomplishments - has founded and run Data-for-Good initiatives.

She'll bring us an insider's perspective on the Data for Good world, INCLUDING a healthy Tech Bro dose of SKEPTICISM.

Join us a bit later than usual this week - Tech Bros are gonna sleep in this Friday - we'll be going live at 4pm PT / 7pm ET.

🍺 Lauren promises to keep the drinking to a minimum even though its officially a Happy Hour show 🍿

🧢 See you then, Bros 🧢

entry.title: [TECHBROS] The Tech Bros talk Tech Sales: Everything you wanted to know but have been afraid to ask
entry.summary:
What did you want to know about Tech Sales, but were too afraid to ask?
🔥 Daring the Tech Bros hot seat this week: Brian Benedict of Kubit 🔥
Brian has led sales and go-to-market teams for over a decade, and will share his 🌶️ takes on:
• What does it take to succeed in tech sales, especially at an early or mid-stage company?
• How is the sales world faring in a downturn?
• What is PLG, and is it going out of fashion?
• Is there now an expectation that every tool and platform include a GPT-4 integration?
• Are we all bots now?
Join us 10am PT / 1pm ET with your questions, 🍿, and 🍺.
As always, Lauren will share her premonitions/ predictions 🔮 about what's about to happen next in data/tech, and Mary will complain about the latest things that annoy her in the wonderful Tech Bro world 🤓
entry.content:
What did you want to know about Tech Sales, but were too afraid to ask?

🔥 Daring the Tech Bros hot seat this week: Brian Benedict of Kubit 🔥

Brian has led sales and go-to-market teams for over a decade, and will share his 🌶️ takes on:

• What does it take to succeed in tech sales, especially at an early or mid-stage company?

• How is the sales world faring in a downturn?

• What is PLG, and is it going out of fashion?

• Is there now an expectation that every tool and platform include a GPT-4 integration?

• Are we all bots now?

Join us 10am PT / 1pm ET with your questions, 🍿, and 🍺.

As always, Lauren will share her premonitions/ predictions 🔮 about what's about to happen next in data/tech, and Mary will complain about the latest things that annoy her in the wonderful Tech Bro world 🤓
